title: Community Shelters * Pregnant Individuals - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Community Shelters * Pregnant Individuals 10 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Ex-Offenders Homeless Couples Homeless Families Homeless Men Homeless Veterans Homeless Women Homeless Youth Mothers Pregnant Individuals Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY COMMUNITY ACTION HILLSBORO FAMILY SHELTER HILLSBORO FAMILY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a year-round shelter that can house families for up to five weeks. Tenant education is available to shelter residents. Residents meet regularly with a family advocate to work on goals, establish a financial plan, and find stable permanent housing. Also provides support for school-aged children to stay in their neighborhood schools. Emergency food boxes are available. 210 SE 12th Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Shelter: Families with minor children and pregnant women. Preference is given to families living on the street and women who are over 8 months pregnant. Food boxes: Unrestricted Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Waitlist: Must be referred by Community Connect Food box: Requests accepted Monday-Friday 5:30pm-9pm, Saturday/Sunday 9am-9pm Email (503) 640-3263 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY PROMISE OF GREATER WASHINGTON COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community shelter. Clients will have access to case management services with the goal of establishing self-sufficiency and eliminating other barriers to permanent housing. 5625 SW Erickson Avenue Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Families with children age 0-18 and pregnant women. Individuals must agree to be drug and alcohol free upon entry and for the duration of the program. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (503) 640-3263 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY PROMISE OF LINCOLN COUNTY FAMILY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter program with supportive services including access to a day center which provides a kitchen, laundry, showers, and a computer work station; case management services for support and resource navigation; and life skills, parenting, financial literacy, nutrition, and second class renters' courses. 5030 SE Highway 101 Lincoln City, OR 97367 Eligibility: Homeless and low-income families with children under age 18 or receiving services from Lincoln County School District and pregnant women. Prospective clients must be clean and sober, and pass a background check. Hours: Intake: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 614-0964 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY PROMISE OF THE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers short-term emergency shelter. Families receive case management and other housing resources, and work to create and follow a plan that builds self-sufficiency that leads toward permanent stability. The program's goal is to help families secure stable, permanent housing within 30 days. This is a faith-inspired program but does not require any affiliation, belief, or attendance at religious services. 1055 Edgewater Street NW Salem, OR 97304 Eligibility: Homeless families (individuals or couples with children under age 18). Pregnant individuals, when space is available. Parents are responsible for supervising their children and following program rules. Must not have active alcohol or drug use, though past use is reviewed on a case-by-case basis. All adults must agree to a criminal background check, with prior convictions considered individually. Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Day Center for clients: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 370-9752 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOOD NEIGHBOR CENTER FAMILY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a family shelter with active assessment and action plans, tenant education, and support. Families have up to 90 days to fulfill their plans and goals. Families have access to their own private rooms. The shelter is equipped with food from community donors for the guests to use to prepare their own meals daily. 11130 SW Greenburg Road Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Pregnant women and families with partial or full custody or guardianship of children under 18 years of age Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 640-3263 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUR JUST FUTURE CHESTNUT TREE INN MOTEL MOTEL SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides waitlist based motel shelter for individuals experiencing homelessness. Eligibility: Current residents of Gresham Women's Shelter screened via a risk assessment Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week for current participants. 11:30pm curfew, but accommodations can be made if job-related. Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUR JUST FUTURE GRESHAM WOMEN'S SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a low-barrier, reservation-based shelter for 90 women, with bunk beds. If unable to access a top bunk, reasonable accommodations may be made if available. Guests will be able to store a small amount of belongings in a secure 3ft locker and use the kitchen, on-site laundry, and showers. Community food storage limited to one cloth grocery bag for cupboard and one gallon size zip bag for fridge per person. Non-perishable food items allowed in personal locker. During the day, guests can use the computer center and access services through referrals with Human Solutions and community partners. Services include assistance finding housing, employment, veterans' services, and mental health and addictions services. There is no time limit on stay at the shelter; guests can stay as long as needed. Eligibility: Female-identified adults age 18 and older. Pregnant women in any trimester of pregnancy. No minors under 18 years old. Top Bunk: Must be able to access a top bunk Bottom Bunk: Bottom bunks are reserved for individuals who are physically unable to access a top bunk. Shelter MAY require documentation from a medical professional stating that a medical restriction prevents access to a top bunk within one week of accessing shelter. One cat or dog is allowed. No other or additional pets are accepted. Pets must have a kennel. Pet can be outside of their kennels while inside the building, as long the owner has control of the animal and it is on a leash. Guests have up to 72 hours to get their own kennel. Hours: Waitlist: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Business hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week for current residents. Curfew is at 11pm. Late entry that is job-related can be arranged during intake. Closed Monday-Friday 12:30pm-3pm for cleaning. Email (971) 333-1223 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PATH HOME FAMILY VILLAGE SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 24/7 shelter in private, locked living and sleeping areas, meals, showers, laundry facilities and on-site supportive services and case management for families as they seek permanent housing. Eligibility: Families with children in their custody under the age of 18 (or plans for reunification) - OR - a third trimester pregnancy (28 weeks or 7 months) with connections to Multnomah County (i.e., was displaced, has kids in school, job, service providers in Multnomah County, etc.) AND meet one of the following criteria: -In an emergency shelter, transitional housing, motel paid for by an agency or voucher or a place not meant for habitation (i.e. car, streets, tent, abandoned building, etc.) -Doubled up (with family or friends and not on the lease, unsafely doubled up or in a living situation that is over safe capacity) and at risk of becoming literally homeless within 14 days -Housed, including self pay at a motel, and will become literally homeless within 14 days or has received a court ordered eviction notice -In an institution (i.e. jail, hospital, substance use or mental health treatment facility, etc.) and at risk of exiting to homelessness within 14 days -Fleeing or attempting to flee domestic violence and lacks the resources or support networks to obtain other safe, permanent housing HOUSEHOLDS ON A LEASE: Families who are currently on a lease and do not have an eviction notice are not eligible. Please seek rent payment assistance. YOUTH: Youth under 18 with children or pregnant are not eligible. Please seek assistance through the Coordinated Entry System for Youth. PETS: Pets allowed. The number and type of pets allowed vary by shelter. If you are offered a shelter space, the caseworker working with you will coordinate the shelter that best suits the family and their pets upon shelter placement. Hours: Callback List for Assessment: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Intake: scheduled by shelter provider after assessment when shelter space is available Shelter: 24/7 211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSEBURG RESCUE MISSION SAMARITAN INN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter with 110 beds including meals, showers, laundry facilities, and clothing. Children must be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian. 544 W Umpqua Street Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Women over age 18, pregnant women, and women with children (boys up to age 14). No pets accepted. Hours: Check in: 6pm for new residents; earlier arrangements can be made if referred by a social service organization, law enforcement, or medical provider Shelter curfew: 6:30pm Meals: Breakfast: Monday-Friday 7am-7:30am, Saturday-Sunday 8am-8:30am Lunch: Monday-Friday noon-12:30pm, Saturday-Sunday 1pm-1:30pm Dinner: 7 days per week 5pm-5:30pm (541) 672-5387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER NIGHT SHELTER ANNEX Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a year-round community shelter that includes a wraparound case management program. Services include access to individual dorm units, laundry, showers, meals, and mail services. 4060 W Amazon Drive Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with children under age 18 and pregnant women (proof of pregnancy required) who are experiencing homelessness Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Intake: 7 days per week 9am-5pm (541) 342-7728 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY HILLSBORO FAMILY SHELTER FAMILY PROMISE OF GREATER WASHINGTON COUNTY FAMILY PROMISE OF GREATER WASHINGTON COUNTY FAMILY PROMISE OF LINCOLN COUNTY FAMILY SHELTER FAMILY PROMISE OF THE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY FAMILY PROMISE OF THE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY GOOD NEIGHBOR CENTER FAMILY SHELTER ROSEBURG RESCUE MISSION SAMARITAN INN ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY NIGHT SHELTER ANNEX
